entry.title: Re: Upload comments through CSV
entry.content:
Make the Comment a dimensionless field in planning model. 

Now every free text can be accepted that gets uploaded. 

To avoid the field after 2, 3, 4 uploads to produce more than 1 row as you probably wish to overwrite you have to Apple that logic in the upload job setting. Since some time there in settings you can choose the replacement behavior and Keys. 

Or use old manual way - upload goes to some edit version (Actual*) 

Or private* Version. And you have a "purge" Button. Or replacement /deletion before reupload happens (data action ... ). 

Make youself familiar wirh "in built comments " , stuff changed there as well heavily that might help (also with AI comment aggregation functions)

entry.title: Re: mm02 attachments in smartforms
entry.content:
Hi @Sandra_Rossi , thanks for your comment, Just to clarify for the OP:

  •  SO_OBJECT_GET_ALL is a standard GOS API for retrieving attachments.
  • SAP_CONVERT_TO_BMP is also a standard FM used when SmartForms require BMP/TIFF formats

These are older but still valid components in many ECC and S/4HANA on‑premise systems, especially in utilities and manufacturing landscapes where GOS attachments are common.

I shared the snippet because it’s one of the practical approaches I’ve used in real projects when customers store material images as MM02 attachments.

Happy to help if the OP needs additional context or alternatives.

entry.title: Re: Hi
entry.content:
Try this: instead of joining the full tables, join only on the document header + line item keys:

define view ZFI_ACDOCA_BSEG as

  select from acdoca as a

    inner join bseg as b

      on  a.rclnt = b.mandt

      and a.bukrs = b.bukrs

      and a.belnr = b.belnr

      and a.gjahr = b.gjahr

      and a.buzei = b.buzei

{

  a.bukrs,

  a.belnr,

  a.gjahr,

  a.buzei,

  a.hkont,

  b.shkzg,

  b.dmbtr

}

entry.title: Re: BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA returns Success but MARC-MAABC and MARC-ABCIN do not update (S/4HANA)
entry.content:
1.  The BAPI requires these structures to be present when updating plant data.

ls_clientdata-material_long = ls_out-matnr.

ls_clientdatax-material_long = 'X'.

2.  Setting only mrp_view = 'X' and storage_view = 'X' is not enough.

ls_headdata-basic_view = 'X'.

ls_headdata-purchase_view = 'X'.

3. If the material is not extended to that plant, the BAPI will not create MARC automatically unless you set:

ls_plantdatax-plant = 'X'.

4. Check the RETURN table for warnings.

entry.title: Is encryption with HSM supported for ASE on IBM AIX?
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We want to implement encryption in our servers using a HSM key, but in version ASE 16.0 SP03 PL06 manual there is a note that mention that AIX and Solaris can not use this functionality.

We are running ASE 16.0 SP04 PL06. Do you know if this restriction continue in this version? 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is encryption with HSM supported for ASE on IBM AIX?
entry.content:
Starting from ASE 16.0 SP03 PL06, SAP ASE can use the Hardware Security Module (HSM) external keystore to encrypt keys and is supported on all SAP ASE platforms except IBM AIX.

At this time, encryption support for IBM AIX remains planned, but SAP has not yet defined an expected release date.

entry.title: Receiving quantities with the TEST RF ENVIRONMENT app, similar to the Receive Produts
entry.content:
The client needs to scan their barcodes to automate the recording of incoming quantities. Currently, EWM is being implemented through the Test RF Environment app; however, it is not handling receiving like the Receive Products app.

Let's look at an example of how the quantities of an item are recorded using the EAN code in the Receive Products APP until the order quantity is completed in S/4HANA PUBLIC EDITION

First, click on Enter ID:

A dialog box called Find a Document appears:

The handling unit 300001057 is placed in the box

Let's simulate scanning the second item, which has the EAN 2050000003820. To do this, we'll click on the barcode icon. You can see that the Received column is blank and contains the UOM CAR.

The following dialog box is displayed and the EAN 2050000003820 is entered

The first scanned amount is displayed, which is why the number 1 is seen in the Received field.

We repeat the process by clicking the scanner icon again; the dialog box then appears, and we enter the EAN 2050000003820 of the item.

It now appears that one more CAR has been scanned; the total number of CAR to be scanned is 6, and since 2 CAR have been scanned, that is why 4 CAR are pending.

In order for the Receive Products app to perform this function, the "GTIN SCAN INCR COUNT" check mark was enabled in the CBC configuration under the "Define Basic Settings for Receive Products" node.

My question is, what is the configuration in CBC or what check mark do I need to enable so that the "TEST RF ENVIRONMENT" app scans by CAR until the order is completed, as shown in the "RECEIVE PRODUCT" app?

In the Maintain User Settings – Radio Frequency application, I already verified my store 5550 and my resource is SHA1-1.

As a first step, upon entering my TEST RF ENVIRONMENT app, the following data is displayed:

Warehouse number: 5550    Resource: SHA1-1   DefPressDvc: SE01    Press Enter.

After Select  03 Inbound Process > 02 Receiving of HUs 

and finally > 01 Rec. Hu By Delivery.

In the Delivery field, enter your delivery number. 180000265.

The incoming delivery is now displayed with the following information:
Delivery: 180000265.
Partner (number and name):1000060
Number of handling units (number of handling units created so far):1
PressEnterto continue.

HU 300001663 was entered and then the press enter.

The product is TRCAME06, and I need to receive 3 CARs. When I click the CHQTY button, as shown in the last image, a field appears to enter the current quantity, but it doesn't accept an EAN like the Receive Products app does.

What configuration allows me to scan each item individually until the order of 3 CARs is complete? I also need it to show me that, for example, 2 CARs have been scanned and 1 CAR is pending to complete the 3-CAR order.

in S/4HANA PUBLIC EDITION

entry.title: Re: How to verify ASE database size and allocation detail...
entry.content:
Database size and allocation details can be obtained through the DBA Cockpit interface, where the information can be accessed without the need for OS‑level commands or isql:

Navigate to DBA Cockpit → Database → Space → Databases

entry.title: RCM - Email notification to the candidate when a field is modified in the Candidate Workbench
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

RCM - Email notification sent to the candidate when a field is updated and the application is saved within the Candidate Workbench at a specific status.

There is a scenario where document verification is performed offline, and the recruiter updates this information in the Candidate Workbench by modifying the corresponding field. Once the document verification is marked as complete in the Candidate Workbench, an email should be sent to the candidate notifying them that the document verification process has been completed.

The email should be linked to the field and triggered based on the change in that field.

We have tried to implement this email trigger using business rules but have been unsuccessful.
Any recommendations or solutions would be highly appreciated.

Best Regards,
Venkatesh.

entry.title: How to Restrict WBS Budget Distribution in CPM Financial Planning?
entry.content:
Restriction Methods

Configuration-Based Restrictions

Status Management Controls

You can exclude specific WBS elements from availability control by activating a user status for the controlling element, where the availability control business transaction is not allowed. If a WBS element has this status, the assigned funds are not included in the availability control, effectively preventing automatic distribution .

Controlling Elements Definition

Define specific controlling elements in the project based on business requirements .Only WBS elements designated as controlling elements will have their own budget control, preventing automatic consumption from parent levels . When costs are posted to WBS elements that are not controlling elements, they consume the budget of the parent WBS .

WBS Structure Controls

Direct Budget Assignment

Assign budget directly to specific WBS elements where you want to restrict distribution. You can assign a WBS element to a program position only if the WBS elements directly above and below it in the hierarchy are not assigned to a program position. This prevents automatic budget flow through the hierarchy.

Budget Transfer Restrictions

To move budget to a superior WBS level, you must first delete the budget on subordinated WBS elements . This manual process prevents automatic distribution and requires deliberate budget management decisions.

System Configuration Steps

  1. Access Status Management: Navigate to Financials → PS-Project System → Structures → Status Management 
  2. Create User Status: Define a user status that excludes availability control business transactions 
  3. Apply Status to WBS: Activate the user status for specific WBS elements where you want to restrict distribution
  4. Define Controlling Elements: Configure which WBS elements should act as controlling elements in the project structure 

Alternative Approaches

Manual Budget Allocation

Use transactions CJ34 (Transfer), CJ35 (Return), CJ36 (Supplement), and CJ37 (Supplement) for manual budget movements between WBS levels . This approach requires explicit approval for any budget distribution.

Budget Profile Configuration

Configure the budget profile to require manual distribution rather than automatic flow-down. Set up the profile to control how cost planning and budgeting are carried out for the work breakdown structure.

Impact Assessment

Before Restriction
  • Budget automatically flows from header WBS to all subordinate levels
  • Lower-level WBS elements can consume parent budget without explicit allocation
  • Availability control checks against parent budget when local budget is unavailable 
After Restriction
  • Budget remains at the assigned WBS level only
  • Lower-level WBS elements cannot access parent budget automatically
  • Each WBS element requires explicit budget assignment for availability control
  • Manual budget transfers required for redistribution 

Validation Steps

  • Use transaction CJ31 and select Extras → Availability Control → Analysis to verify budget consumption patterns 
  • Check the "Distributable" column in budget transactions to confirm distribution restrictions 
  • Test posting costs to lower-level WBS elements to ensure they cannot exceed their specific budget allocations
  • Run report BPFCTRA0 to analyze controlling elements and their budget behavior 

Considerations and Risks

Process Changes Required
  • Budget planning processes must be redesigned to accommodate manual distribution
  • Users need training on new budget transfer procedures using transactions CJ34-CJ37 
  • Additional approval workflows may be necessary for budget movements
System Performance
  • Manual budget management increases administrative overhead
  • More granular budget control requires additional monitoring and reporting
  • Potential for budget bottlenecks if distribution approvals are delayed
Planning Flexibility
  • Reduced flexibility in dynamic budget reallocation during project execution
  • May require more frequent budget revision cycles to accommodate changing project needs
  • Could impact project agility if budget restrictions are too rigid

entry.title: SSL Certificate - SAP Enable Now - OnPremise
entry.content:
Overview of SAP Enable Now (On-Premise)

SAP Enable Now (On-Premise) is a training and user support solution that helps organizations educate employees on how to use SAP and other business applications effectively.

In an on-premise setup, the system is installed on the company’s own servers and managed internally rather than hosted in the cloud. This approach gives the organization complete control over data security, access management, and system configuration. It is particularly suitable for companies with strict IT policies or regulatory requirements.

SAP Enable Now allows organizations to:

  • Develop training content such as simulations, user guides, and videos
  • Provide real-time, step-by-step guidance within applications
  • Centrally manage and update learning materials
  • Support user adoption during system implementations or upgrades

Overall, it improves user productivity, minimizes errors, and ensures smoother system adoption across the organization.

Why SSL is Required for SAP Enable Now (On-Premise)

Implementing an SSL certificate is essential to secure communication between users and the SAP Enable Now server.

SSL ensures that:

  • Data transmitted between users and the server is encrypted
  • Login credentials and sensitive information are protected
  • Users do not encounter browser security warnings
  • The system complies with corporate security standards

Procedure:

1. Go to Command Prompt from the path of bin in JAVA folder

2. Enter the below command with dname "CN=<> , OU=<> , O=<> , L=<> , ST=<> , C=<>"

Fill the relevant details inside <>

3. Make sure to Enter the Keystore Password, upon entering, Keystore will be generated.

4. Create the Certificate Request by entering the below command with the created keystore path and password.

5. Check the CSR generated in any online decoder site

6. Edit the Server.xml file in Tomcat -> Conf -> Server.xml

7. Upon receiving the Certificate, import the certificate response by using the below command.

Enter the Certificate path in -trustcacerts

8. Please confirm

Enabling SSL on SAP Enable Now (On-Premise) is a critical step to secure communication between users and the server. SSL encrypts data transmitted across the network, protecting sensitive information such as login credentials and training content from unauthorized access.

Beyond security, SSL also ensures users do not encounter browser warnings and helps the system comply with organizational IT policies and regulatory requirements.

In short, enabling SSL enhances data protection, strengthens user trust, and ensures a secure and compliant SAP Enable Now environment.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: ksef portal is not accepting sap generated xml file
entry.content:
Good day @jerrysam 

Thank you for your question.

That message simply means you are not uploading a valid XML e invoice file, or it was changed and therefore it no longer matches it.

Note that SAP offers various XML files e.g., SAP eDocument internal XML, Output XML (not KSeF Fa structure), KSeF structured invoice XML (FA), etc.

I suggest you check the following:

  • Generate the file using the Poland KSeF einvoices process in eDocument Cockpit/Manage Electronic Documents, not from a general output or spool XML. See the following reference for more details: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb525a45b55f4a1c9bb32c254affeeec/750aa653238a4144ac15c7107a38307d.html
  • Confirm which FA schema your configuration is using. Either (FA(2) or FA(3) and validate your XML against the exact XSD the portal expects. Pls check note - 3346323 - eDocument Poland B2B(KSeF) Troubleshooting Guide
  • Confirm you do not have technical validation failure.
    • Open the XML in Notepad++ and confirm it is UTF 8 and has a proper XML header.
    • Validate your XML file against the official FA XSD with either MF or any XSD validator
    • If it fails, then fix the data with eDocument generation, not by editing your XML manually.
  • Make sure your file did not get altered after you downloaded it. Upload it without opening and resaving it. You can also compare file hash before and after the transfer is concluded.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: MM Pricing conditions - Cannot copy condition in supp...
entry.content:
Good day @ornecogli 

Thank you for your question.

What you described is consistent with how (PO --> SES --> Supplier) service procurement works. The supplier invoice amount is derived from the SES service values, accrual price components are typically treated as relevant, but they are not transferred as pricing lines for LIV that are relevant for the invoice. The reason behind that is:

  • SES posts the accrual correctly
  • Supplier invoice does not bring the condition line, and the invoice amount does not match PO or SES.

See: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3532883 for more details on Accruals condition not behaving like net price components in purchasing.

Here is a quick check before proceeding:

  • In your PO, open item pricing details and confirm whether your two red conditions are contributing to the net price or shown only as a component.
  • In your supplier invoice item, check if the invoice is SES based.
  • If invoice is SES based, any pricing conditions that are not part of the SES service rule will not be taken as invoice line.

That confirmed, here is my suggestion:

In Public Cloud, you can model them as Planned Delivery Costs or separate invoice relevant charges. If your “plastic tax” and “duties” must be part of your vendor invoices and must appear in MIRO, do not use the V condition category. Instead:

  • Change Condition Category in your custom condition types from V to a standard invoice relevant condition category (blank/normal condition) so it becomes part of your invoice value.
  • Make sure your condition is not statistical and is invoice relevant and keep account determination for the posting if you need separate GL lines.

Alternatively, you can add separate service lines for tax and duties for “Plastic tax” and “Duties”. Ensure your quantity and price as fixed amount lines, approve SED with those lines included and post your supplier invoice referencing to SES.

You may also consider handling “plastic tax” via the tax procedure/tax code and not as manual MM pricing conditions if it is statutory tax and must be reported as one.

To summarise:

  • In service procurement, the invoice amount usually comes from SES values.
  • Your conditions are set as price component with Accruals.
  • This setup can post accruals at SES, but the condition is often not invoice relevant, so MIRO does not bring the condition line. Making totals differ from PO and SES.
  • Add plastic tax and duties as separate service lines so they flow PO --> SES --> Invoice or redesign your condition types to be invoice relevant and avoid condition category V.

These references provide some guidance:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/98e16c4a681447e89c13cc8670a5a617.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/98e16c4a681447e89c13cc8670a5a617.html

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: OData v2 upsert on custom MDF Object creates record but does NOT trigger workflow
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm trying to create a new record in a custom MDF object (cust_mymdf) via the OData v2 API using a POST to the /upsert endpoint, with the goal of triggering the configured approval workflow.

What I'm trying to achieve:

  • Use the OData API to insert a termination request record as pending data.
  • Have the system's standard MDF workflow (configured on the object with a workflow group for approval) automatically trigger upon creation via API, so an approver receives a notification and can review/approve/reject.

Setup details:

  • Custom MDF object: cust_mymdf (effective-dated, externalCode as key field).
  • Pending Data = Yes on the object definition.
  • Workflow is configured directly on the object (have also tried business rule on save).
  • API user:   (non-admin/technical user, no "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission granted in RBP).
  • Authentication: Basic Auth (username (myuser@companyid) + password).

API call (via Postman):

  • Method: POST
  • URL:https://api55preview.sapsf.eu/odata/v2/upsert?workflowConfirmed=true
  • Body (JSON):
{
  "__metadata": {
    "uri": "cust_mymdf(effectiveStartDate=datetime'2026-02-19T00:00:00',externalCode='19')",
    "type": "SFOData.cust_mymdf"
  },
  "externalCode": "19",
  "effectiveStartDate": "/Date(1771459200000)/",
  "externalName": "TestTerminate19Update",
  "cust_Employee": "B200394",
  "cust_TerminationDate": "/Date(1771459200000)/",
  "cust_OkToRehire": "Y",
  "cust_DeactivatePosition": "N"
}

Result:

  • HTTP 200 OK
  • Response body:
{
  "d": [
    {
      "key": "cust_mymdf/effectiveStartDate=2026-02-19T00:00:00.000Z,cust_mymdf/externalCode=19",
      "status": "OK",
      "editStatus": "UPSERTED",
      "message": null,
      "index": 0,
      "httpCode": 200,
      "inlineResults": null
    }
  ]
}

 

  • The record appears in Manage Data
  • No workflow is triggered — no approval request is created, no notification sent, nothing appears in Manage Workflow Requests or the approver's inbox.

    What I've already checked / tried:

    • The parameter ?workflowConfirmed=true is included in the URL (as required per sap documentation to simulate UI confirmation).
    • The API user does not have the "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission.
    • Manual creation via UI (Manage Data → Create new record for cust_MassTerminate) does trigger the workflow correctly (approver sees it).
    • Effective date is future-dated (2026-02-19), but same behavior occurs if I test with current date.
    • Workflow is set to trigger on create.
    • No duplicate pending records on the same key.

      According to SAP KBA 2396714 ("Workflow not Triggered via API or Integration Center") and community blogs (e.g., "Triggering workflows for MDF using OData API"), the combination of:

      • workflowConfirmed=trueand
      • API user without "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission

      should allow the workflow to trigger via API — but it's not happening in my case. Has anyone successfully triggered a custom MDF workflow via OData upsert in this scenario? Any additional prerequisites, payload tweaks, or known limitations I'm missing? Any help or pointers would be greatly appreciated — thank you!

entry.title: RAP – Early Numbering Triggered When Clicking Create Button on Object Page (Fields Not Displayed)
entry.content:
Scenario

I am developing a RAP based Fiori Object Page application for a Contract Header entity. The Contract Number is generated using a Number Range object (ZCONTRACT) in the earlynumbering_create method.

Expected Behavior

When the user clicks the Create button:
• The Object Page should open in draft mode
• Input fields should appear empty or editable
• Number generation should happen only when appropriate (after user interaction/save)

Actual Behavior

When the user clicks Create:
• The system immediately executes the earlynumbering_create  method
• A contract number is generated instantly
• The Object Page does not show the input fields correctly
• After that, no further processing happens

Technical Details

I am using:
• Managed RAP Behavior
• Draft enabled application
• Early numbering implementation
• Custom helper class for buffering database operations

The Contract Number is generated using FM  NUMBER_GET_NEXT inside earlynumbering_create.

How can I prevent the number range from being triggered immediately when the Create button is pressed?

I want the Object Page to first display the editable input fields in draft mode and then generate the number at the correct stage of processing.  Is my early numbering implementation incorrect, or should I use late numbering instead?

Below is the relevant code:

METHOD create.
    DATA:ls_con TYPE zcontract_head,
         lv_time TYPE abp_lastchange_tstmpl.

    LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(ls_enti).

      SELECT SINGLE contract_no FROM zcontract_head INTO @DATA(lv_conno) WHERE contract_no = @LS_enti-ContractNo.

      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
       GET TIME STAMP FIELD lv_time.
       ls_con-contract_no = ls_enti-ContractNo.
       ls_con-purch_org   = ls_enti-PurchOrg.
       ls_con-doc_date    = ls_enti-DocDate.
       ls_con-expiry_date = ls_enti-ExpiryDate.
       ls_con-risk_score  = ls_enti-RiskScore.
       ls_con-status      = ls_enti-Status.
       ls_con-vendor      = '100100'.
       ls_con-create_by   = sy-uname.
       ls_con-create_on   = sy-datum.
       ls_con-total_value = '63000'.
       ls_con-currency    = 'INR'.
       ls_con-changed_by  = SY-uname.
       ls_con-changed_on  = lv_time.
       DATA(lobj) = zcl_contract_helper=>get_instance( ).
            lobj->add_create( EXPORTING ls_data = ls_con ).

       APPEND VALUE #(
                      %cid =  ls_enti-%cid
                      ContractNo =  ls_enti-ContractNo
                      %is_draft  =  ls_enti-%is_draft
                     ) TO mapped-z_cds_view_contract_header.

     ELSE.
      APPEND VALUE #(
                      %cid =  ls_enti-%cid
                      ContractNo =  ls_enti-ContractNo
                      %is_draft  =  ls_enti-%is_draft
                    ) TO failed-z_cds_view_contract_header.

      APPEND VALUE #(
        %cid = ls_enti-%cid
        %msg = new_message(
                 id       = 'ZGLC_MSG'
                 number   = '016'
                 severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error )
                          ) TO reported-z_cds_view_contract_header.
     ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.


METHOD earlynumbering_create.
  DATA: lv_number      TYPE n LENGTH 6,
        lv_year        TYPE string,
        lv_contractno  TYPE string.

  LOOP AT entities ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_entity>).

    CALL FUNCTION 'NUMBER_GET_NEXT'
      EXPORTING
        nr_range_nr = '01'
        object      = 'ZCONTRACT'
      IMPORTING
        number      = lv_number
      EXCEPTIONS
        OTHERS      = 1.

    IF sy-subrc = 0 AND lv_number IS NOT INITIAL.

      lv_year = sy-datum(4).

      lv_contractno = |CTR-{ lv_year }-{ lv_number WIDTH = 6 PAD = '0' }|.

      APPEND VALUE #(
                     %cid       = <fs_entity>-%cid
                     ContractNo = lv_contractno
                     %is_draft = <fs_entity>-%is_draft
                    ) TO mapped-z_cds_view_contract_header.

    ELSE.

      APPEND VALUE #(
                     %cid      = <fs_entity>-%cid
                     %is_draft = <fs_entity>-%is_draft
                    ) TO failed-z_cds_view_contract_header.

      APPEND VALUE #(
                     %cid     = <fs_entity>-%cid
                     %is_draft = <fs_entity>-%is_draft
                     %msg     = new_message(
                     id       = 'ZGLC_MSG'
                     number   = '015'
                     severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error )
                    ) TO reported-z_cds_view_contract_header.

    ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.


METHOD save.
    DATA(lobj_save) = zcl_contract_helper=>get_instance(  ).
         lobj_save->save(  ).
  ENDMETHOD.

METHOD cleanup.
    DATA(lobj_clear) = zcl_contract_helper=>get_instance(  ).
        lobj_clear->clear_buffer(  ).
  ENDMETHOD.

CLASS zcl_contract_helper DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

 PUBLIC SECTION.

    CLASS-METHODS:
      get_instance
        RETURNING VALUE(ro_instance)
        TYPE REF TO zcl_contract_helper.

    METHODS:
      add_create IMPORTING ls_data TYPE zcontract_head,
      add_delete IMPORTING lv_contract_no TYPE zcontract_head-contract_no,
      save,
      clear_buffer.

  PRIVATE SECTION.

    CLASS-DATA:
      glo_instance TYPE REF TO zcl_contract_helper.

    DATA:
      gt_create TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zcontract_head,
      gt_delete TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zcontract_head-contract_no.

ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_contract_helper IMPLEMENTATION.
 METHOD get_instance.
    IF glo_instance IS INITIAL.
      CREATE OBJECT glo_instance.
    ENDIF.
    ro_instance = glo_instance.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD add_create.
    IF ls_data IS NOT INITIAL.
      APPEND ls_data TO gt_create.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD add_delete.
    IF lv_contract_no IS NOT INITIAL.
      APPEND lv_contract_no TO gt_delete.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD save.
    IF gt_create IS NOT INITIAL.
      MODIFY zcontract_head FROM TABLE gt_create.
    ENDIF.

    LOOP AT gt_delete INTO DATA(lv_del).
      DELETE FROM zcontract_head
        WHERE contract_no = lv_del.
    ENDLOOP.

    clear_buffer( ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD clear_buffer.
    CLEAR: gt_create, gt_delete.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: Fix pdfmake Table Width Issues with Long Continuous Text and Special Characters (SAP UI5)
entry.content:
Awesome write-up! 🙌 This pdfmake table width edge case with continuous strings is something a lot of developers don’t catch until it hits them in real data—really well explained.

I especially liked how you dug into why pdfmake fails to wrap long unbroken text and the clear explanation that it sees a long string as one unbreakable unit, so fixed column widths don’t work without break points. The zero-width space (\u200B) trick to insert invisible wrap points is a clever and practical workaround that doesn’t affect the visual output but keeps the layout intact—great for real-world SAP UI5 PDF features! 👏

Thanks for sharing a working demo as well—this will definitely save others time debugging similar PDF issues. Looking forward to more insights like this! 🚀

entry.title: Re: BOM Status with Change Number
entry.content:
Good day @nuno_pedroso1 

Thank you for your question.

Status 1 means that the BOM is active and can be selected by MRP and production order processing without requiring Engineering Change Management history handling. It is the typical status for normal production usage (as long as the BOM is valid on the required date). See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ERP/85d3fce10e264972a0155c8b46ecf93b/d5abce5314894208e10000000a174cb4.html

Status 3 “Active with History Requirement” stands for ECM controlled BOM changes. It basically means the BOM is still active, but its effectivity and change number validity become critical for planning and execution. If the BOM is not considered valid on the relevant date, the system can fail to find a valid BOM for the production version and then your order creation fails. See KBA 3435592, Change Number Object Management Records - Material BOM and Managing Material BOM Changes with a Change Number

The error “Nov valid production version found....C2142” with Status 3 is raised when the system cannot find a production version that is valid for the order dates and lot size, or when the BOM that belongs to the production version is not valid on the key date. With Status 3, “BOM validity” is often impacted by change number effectively.

So, when you switch the BOM back to Status 1, the system typically finds the BOM as valid again, so the production version selection succeeds, and the order can be created.

Here is a quick guide:

  • Verify your BOM validity and status. If you used a change number, confirm the change number master has the correct valid-from date that matches your required production or order date. See https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3435592 for more insights.

  • Check the Production Version that should select this BOM, filter by material and plant. Then in your relevant production version, verify validity dates, Lot size range and BOM alternative assigned is the one you expect.

  • If you use change numbers, verify the ECM effectively consistency. In your Change Master app, open the change number used for the BOM change, verify valid-from date is not in the future relative to production order dates and the change number is not restricting the BOM from being effective on the order date. See Managing Material BOM Changes with a Change Number for more insights.

  • Then retest your scenario by creating the order from the same material, plant and date. If it still fails with C2142, then adjust either Production version validity/lot size, or change number validity/effectivity dates so the BOM is valid on the order date.

Regarding the use of Status 3 in Public Cloud, this is used when you want:

  • Controlled BOM changes with change numbers.
  • History tracking and date effectively that must be enforced consistently.

However, it requires that:

  • You align your BOM and change number validity with production dates, and
  • You maintain your production versions with matching validity and lot size ranges.

To summarise:

  • Status 1 is simpler and commonly used for production BOM selection.
  • Status 3 is for ECM controlled BOMs and makes validity and change number effectively critical.
  • Your C2142 is happening because the production version cannot find a BOM that is valid on the order date when BOM is Status3, usually due to change number or validity mismatch. See https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2641006 for more details.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Trying to reconcile CO09 with BAPI_MATERIAL_AVAILABILITY
entry.content:
@spinellll I just received a feedback from the moderators. The fact that you accepted the answer prevents them to moderate this answer although it's clearly produced by GenAI, either see the first version of the answer (version history) which is full of "** bleep **" (whatever it means), or see the result generated by Copilot below.

So I'm asking you to help the community and unaccept the solution.

Hi Leo,

Thanks for the attachment — that helps a lot. Based on what’s shown in your CO09, the quantities returned by BAPI_MATERIAL_AVAILABILITY do correspond directly to values visible in CO09, but they are not on the “Totals record” lines. They are on the batch-level ATP lines.

Here’s how to reconcile them step by step.

1. What BAPI_MATERIAL_AVAILABILITY is returning

From your screenshots:

  • Batch MIXTEST1 → COM_QTY = 880
  • Batch 04221901 → COM_QTY = 100

These are ATP-confirmable quantities per batch, calculated using the checking rule you passed to the BAPI.

2. Where to find these quantities in CO09

In CO09, you need to look at the ATP situation section and focus on the batch stock (BtchSt / BtchLoc) lines, not the summary lines.

Batch MIXTEST1 → 880

On the CO09 screen:

  • Look for the line with:
    • MRP element: BtchLoc
    • MRP element data: MIXTEST1
  • On that line:
    • The “**bleep**. ATP qty” column shows 880

This is exactly the same number returned by the BAPI as COM_QTY = 880.

Important:
The higher numbers you see above (like 1,073 / 1,164) belong to TotRec / TotReq / MRP totals and are not batch-specific. The BAPI is not returning those.

Batch 04221901 → 100

Similarly:

  • Find the line with:
    • MRP element: BtchSt or BtchLoc
    • MRP element data: 04221901
  • On that line:
    • The “Rec./reqd qty” and “**bleep**. ATP qty” show 100

This matches BAPI_MATERIAL_AVAILABILITY → COM_QTY = 100.

3. Why this often looks confusing

A few common pitfalls when comparing CO09 and the BAPI:

  1. Totals record vs. batch lines

    • CO09 defaults to showing aggregated totals first.
    • The BAPI returns batch-level ATP, which only appears on the BtchSt / BtchLoc rows.

  2. Confirmed vs. **bleep**. ATP

    • The BAPI COM_QTY aligns with **bleep**. ATP qty, not with:
      • “Confirmed” column on totals
      • MRP receipt quantities

  3. Checking rule consistency

    • As long as:
      • Same checking rule
      • Same plant
      • Same material
      • Same batch
    • The numbers will reconcile exactly at the batch ATP line.

4. One quick visual rule of thumb

If you want to “point” to the number on CO09 that matches the BAPI result:

✅ Always point to the “**bleep**. ATP qty” on the BtchSt / BtchLoc line for that batch

Not the totals, not the material-level rows.

entry.title: Re: Improving the SAP Community together, one comment at a time
entry.content:
@qmacro  You're right; the community needs to take action to counter the false information in the comments.
But if a multi-billion-dollar company isn't doing its part, why are we being called upon to do it?

Ironically, community posts are actually used and sold as part of the SAP product Joule for Consultants.
There should actually be a particularly strong interest in ensuring that these posts are reasonably accurate.
When we try to improve quality as a community by reporting AI-generated posts containing false information, because a comment simply isn't enough, we have to justify or explain why the post doesn't belong on the platform.
If even @Sandra_Rossi  is no longer interested, then you all need to sit down and decide which direction you want to go in. If things don't change, all the long-standing community members who are interested in quality will leave.

entry.title: Re: Improving the SAP Community together, one comment at a time
entry.content:
@Sandra_Rossi if only you knew how much time we have spent debating all these AI-posts on our end 😅

Our problem is that we cannot undeniably prove that someone has used GenAI and that the posts have zero value. When we try to educate the users, we often end up in endless/meaningless debates with them because they claim they have not used AI at all, and as I said, we often cannot undeniably prove that they have. In almost cases we see now the content is also no longer 100% AI-generated but is a combination of AI- and human text. 

In the case you meantion in your comment here, the user who asked the question marked the answer as the correct solution, meaning that, although the answer was AI-generated it provided value and helped him.

If there is nothing misleading or false, and the generated answers are marked as the correct solution, what do you suggest we should do?

entry.title: Re: mm02 attachments in smartforms
entry.content:
Lovely discussion here 🙂
This illustrates what can happen with the use of AI without validating for actual sources.

Whether it is a hallucination or not, I cannot find it in S/4 2023 and neither on the S/4 2025 sandbox system, both are on-premise systems.
The code does not look like it makes much sense to me to be honest.

For the original question:
It has been years since I have worked with SMARTFORMS, but I did have images added in adobe forms.
Perhaps this logic can be of use to you @Manoj_B_Gaikwad .
It has some warehousing specific logic in it because it is for a warehouse output form.
Nonetheless, the concept to get the gos info will be similar if not the same.
The method returns the image url as well as image stream, which is the binary data.
I think one of those can be used on the SAMRTFORM but am not sure about that, alternatively you might have to store the binary in the system as an image (color BMP) temporary for use in the SMARTFORMS.

Her is my logic for adobe form that served me well.
I know it is using OPEN DATASET, which is not valid for ABAP Cloud language, but it should not immidiately be a problem for private cloud or on-premise.

 

METHOD get_product_dms_doc.
    DATA lt_documentlist    TYPE TABLE OF bapi_doc_keys.

    "Get url
    DATA(lv_objectkey)      = CONV objky_50( iv_product ).
    DATA(lv_objectkey_long) = CONV objky_90( iv_product ).
    CLEAR lt_documentlist[].

* Check Document Info Records with Warehouse Link.
    SELECT warehouse , documenttype , documentpart , isdefault
    FROM  zv_zv_dir_plant_link
    WHERE warehouse = @iv_lgnum
    AND documenttype = @iv_documenttype
    ORDER BY isdefault DESCENDING
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_dir_plant_link).
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      CLEAR : lt_dir_plant_link .
    ENDIF.

    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_DOCUMENT_GETOBJECTDOCS'
      EXPORTING
        objecttype     = 'MARA'
        objectkey      = lv_objectkey
        objectkey_long = lv_objectkey_long
      TABLES
        documentlist   = lt_documentlist.
    TRY .

        READ TABLE lt_dir_plant_link ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_dir_plant_link>)
        WITH KEY warehouse = iv_lgnum
                 documenttype  = iv_documenttype
                 isdefault = ''.
        IF sy-subrc = 0 .
          DATA(ls_dl) = lt_documentlist[ documenttype = iv_documenttype documentpart = <ls_dir_plant_link>-documentpart ].
        ENDIF.
        IF ls_dl IS INITIAL.
          READ TABLE lt_dir_plant_link ASSIGNING <ls_dir_plant_link>
          WITH KEY warehouse = iv_lgnum
                   documenttype  = iv_documenttype
                   isdefault = 'X'.
          IF sy-subrc = 0 .
            ls_dl = lt_documentlist[ documenttype = iv_documenttype documentpart = <ls_dir_plant_link>-documentpart ].
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.


        " get image from AL11
        """"""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""
        DATA lt_doc_file TYPE TABLE OF bapi_doc_files2.
        CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_DOCUMENT_GETDETAIL2'
          EXPORTING
            documenttype    = ls_dl-documenttype
            documentnumber  = ls_dl-documentnumber
            documentpart    = ls_dl-documentpart
            documentversion = ls_dl-documentversion
          TABLES
            documentfiles   = lt_doc_file.

        DATA: lt_file_data TYPE TABLE OF x255,
              ls_line      TYPE x255.
        REFRESH lt_file_data.

        DATA(lv_file) = lt_doc_file[ 1 ]-docfile.
        ev_url = lv_file.

        "Get file data
        OPEN DATASET lv_file FOR INPUT IN BINARY MODE.
        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          DO.                                            "#EC CI_NESTED
            READ DATASET lv_file INTO ls_line.
            IF sy-subrc = 0.
              APPEND ls_line TO lt_file_data.
            ELSE.
              APPEND ls_line TO lt_file_data.
              EXIT.
            ENDIF.
          ENDDO.
          CLOSE DATASET lv_file.
        ENDIF.
        LOOP AT lt_file_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_file_data>). "#EC CI_NESTED
          ev_stream = ev_stream && <ls_file_data>.
        ENDLOOP.

      CATCH cx_sy_itab_line_not_found.

    ENDTRY.
  ENDMETHOD.

 

I hope it can at least be of some use to you.

entry.title: Re: Multiple PR's are getting generated from the Work Ord...
entry.content:
Here are the most critical attributes that must be identical for items to be grouped into one PR:

  • Account Assignment Category (e.g., cost center, order, asset)
  • Account Assignment Details (same cost center, order number, WBS element, etc.)
  • Purchasing Group
  • Material Group
  • Vendor (if source of supply is fixed)
  • Plant
  • Company Code
  • Delivery Date (must be identical; even one-day difference causes a split)
  • Requisitioner/User ID
  • Storage Location (if relevant)
  • Item Category (in your case, all ‘N’)
  • Currency

Why This Matters in Your Case
Even though vendor and material group are the same, differences in delivery dates, account assignment, requisitioner, or storage location often cause multiple PRs. For non-stock items, the system is especially strict because each line item is tied to a specific order/account assignment.

  • How to Troubleshoot
    Compare the generated PRs line by line — look for subtle differences in the above fields.
  • If you want consolidation despite differences (e.g., different delivery dates but still one PR), you’ll need a custom enhancement (commonly via BAdI ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST).
  • You can implement logic to force grouping by vendor/material group, overriding SAP’s standard checks.

entry.title: Re: What is the recommended learning roadmap for an SAP S...
entry.content:
Dear @bryan_mendoza71,

Question 1.

Core must-do:

1. Administrating SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
Your first anchor course for Public Edition administration fundamentals launchpad/admin basics, access setup, key operational tasks

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/administrating-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition

2. Introducing SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS/IPS)
Essential for IAM in Public Cloud authentication, SSO concepts and provisioning lifecycle foundations

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/introducing-sap-cloud-identity-services

3. Operating with SAP Cloud ALM (Operations)
Core for monitoring + operations capabilities, use cases and hands-on with the Cloud ALM operations demo system, also relevant to automation Cloud ALM includes Intelligent Event Processing and operations automation integration concepts

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/operating-with-sap-cloud-alm

https://learning.sap.com/courses/operating-with-sap-cloud-alm/explaining-sap-cloud-alm-for-operations-in-a-nutshell

4. Discovering SAP Activate Implementation Tools and Methodology
Gives you SAP official playbook and connects methodology + toolchain (incl. SAP Cloud ALM introduction and workstreams)

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discovering-sap-activate-implementation-tools-and-methodology

https://learning.sap.com/courses/discovering-sap-activate-implementation-tools-and-methodology/accessing-content

Next layer when you own integrations + extensibility governance

5. Managing Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, admin-level important because Public Cloud upgrades apply continuously and extensions/integrations must be cloud-compliant clean core principles

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/managing-clean-core-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud

Question 2. (Most of the links are above, I will post new ones in the phases below)

Phase 1: Foundations

Outcome: You can run the tenant safely and explain the Public Cloud admin scope/limits

  • Administrating SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Introducing SAP Cloud Identity Services
  • Introducing / Operating with SAP Cloud ALM start with intro then operations depth

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/introducing-sap-cloud-alm

Phase 2: Practitioner

Outcome: You can run IAM and operations in a controlled, audit-friendly way

  • Deepen IAM identity provisioning + SSO patterns, access lifecycle processes
  • Operate with Cloud ALM set up monitoring and alert routing understand scope process, integration, jobs, security analysis, health
  • SAP Activate tooling view how SAP expects you to run implementations and continuous change

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/monitoring-integrations-with-sap-cloud-alm

Phase 3: Advanced

Outcome: You become the person who prevents outages and upgrade surprises

  • Integration admin SAP Integration Suite learning journey for API Mgmt + integration patterns
  • Clean core governance what must stay standard, what can be extended, how to keep upgrades safe
  • Side-by-side extensibility on BTP security, deployment, integration into SAP Build Work Zone

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-with-sap-integration-suite

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/building-side-by-side-extensions-on-sap-btp

Question 3.

For an S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Administrator the best-value certifications tend to prove you can run the platform services around the SaaS:

1. SAP Certified SAP BTP Administrator
High value if you touch subaccounts, security, connectivity, and platform operations

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-sap-btp-administrator

2. SAP Certified Solution Transformation Consultant with SAP Cloud ALM
Best fit if you own ALM/operations governance, monitoring, and implementation-to-operations practices

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-solution-transformation-consultant-with-sap-cloud-alm

3. SAP Certified Associate Integration Developer
Very valuable if you own integrations, API governance, troubleshooting and interface reliability

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-integration-developer

Question 4.

Priority technical:

1. IAM mastery SSO + provisioning + role design patterns least privilege, audit readiness

2. Cloud ALM operations monitoring, alerting, event routing, job/automation monitoring, integration and exception monitoring

3. Integration operations Communication arrangements/authentication approach, API lifecycle basics, monitoring/troubleshooting

4. Clean core + extensibility governance upgrade-safe design, side-by-side where appropriate

You don’t need to be a full functional consultant, but you do need enough to:

  • map business processes to monitoring objects
  • understand impact of authorization changes
  • reason about integration payloads and errors

Question 5.

  • Build a portfolio runbook, IAM architecture diagram, monitoring dashboards, integration standards, extension decision matrix
  • Treat each Public Cloud release as a learning cycle impact, test and adapt this is where Clean Core + Cloud ALM pays off
  • Use SAP Activate as your structure it’s the easiest way to communicate professionally with project teams and customers

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: How to Integrate ABAP Composite Roles in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
When you're integrating launchpad content from SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to SAP Build Work Zone and have an existing role model built on composite roles in your ABAP system, you've likely encountered the challenge of efficiently exposing these roles. The good news is there are two ways to expose composite roles from your ABAP backend to Work Zone, using the Common Data Model (CDM) content exposure. In this post we will break down what your options are, each with distinct technical implications.

Option 1: Direct Exposure of Composite Roles

With this approach, you select the composite role itself for exposure in the ABAP transaction /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE (Launchpad Content Exposure to SAP BTP). The content of all underlying single roles is aggregated into the selected composite role, which is then exposed as single role to SAP BTP.

Key points

  • The consumer (Work Zone) sees the composite role as a single, unified role containing all the launchpad content of its component single roles.
  • The individual single roles inside the composite are not exposed separately.
  • Should you require separate access to the single roles beyond the unified composite role, you'll need to manually include each single role in the exposure selection.
  • Exposed roles are available in the BTP cockpit and can be assigned to users. However, IPS can optionally be used to automate role collection assignments and reduce administrative effort.
  • Direct exposure is available since SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition release 2023 or release 2022 with note 3325870

Option 2: Indirect Exposure via Single Roles (with IPS)

The second approach keeps your original role structure and the IPS ensures correct user to role assignment between the ABAP system and BTP.
You select only the single roles which contain launchpad content for exposure in /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE (not the composite role itself).
During content calculation, the system checks whether these single roles belong to any composite roles. If they do, it includes a reference to this relation in the exposed CDM content. The composite role itself is not exposed to Work Zone.

IPS handles the role assignment logic, ensuring users with composite roles get the correct roles assigned on BTP side.

Key points

  • Each exposed single role includes information about its composite role relationships, but those composite roles are not directly available in the Content Manager on BTP.
  • The Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) ensures that when an user is assigned to a composite role in ABAP, all required single roles (now exposed) are assigned in Work Zone/BTP. For further information see  Configure Integration with the Identity Provisioning Service and Exploring Role Assignments
  • If you expose only some single roles from a composite role, any other single roles within that composite are not automatically included. Users will only see apps from the exposed single roles in Work Zone not the full composite role content.
  • Indirect exposure is available since SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition release 2022.

Next Steps

Both approaches are technically valid and work with CDM exposure version 1 and version 2.

If you're invested in IPS-driven provisioning the indirect approach gives you flexibility for Work Zone integration by keeping the existing role structure and automated user to role provisioning. Without IPS you have the option to directly expose composite roles and maintain user to role assignment on BTP side. 

In any case avoid creating dedicated roles solely for exposure purposes. Instead, use the same roles that are actively used by your local entry point (SAP Fiori Launchpad). This ensures consistency between your on-premise and cloud experiences, reduces administrative overhead, and maintains a single source of truth for your role-based content model.

entry.title: Integrating Using Google Cloud Storage Adapter (GCP Authentication & adapter config)
entry.content:
Google Storage Adapter

Introduction to GCS (Google Cloud Storage) :

GCS is a robust and RESTful service for storing and retrieving the data like documents, videos and photos. It stores as a object and provides high performance designed specifically for enterprises. GCS also provide encryption/ decryption capabilities. Our Integration suites provides a adapter to connect with GCS in accessing the object. In this blog, we are going to see how to create service account in Google cloud platform and use it in SAP integration suite for authentication and basic configuration for uploading and retrieving a file.

Advantages of using GCS Adapter: 

  • Integration suite has built-in for GCS adapter, so no need to manually construct REST endpoints.
  • Built-in support for Buckets and Object operation.
  • No manual OAuth token handling and no custom scripts for token generation.
  • Config driven Object operations like upload (Put Object), Download (Get Object), Delete Object and List Objects.
  • Few integration steps and faster development.
  • Built-in Error handling and Validation. The adapter Validates bucket and Object Configuration.
  • Dynamic File and Path Handling. You can dynamically define Buckets. Object Paths and File names.
  • Key security benefits includes secure key storage and compliance with SAP security Standards

How to get Create Service Account ?

Step 1:  Create Service Account in GCP

  • Navigate to  IAM & Admin -> Service Accounts.
  • Click service account and give a name and add required roles. (You need ‘Storage Object Admin’ role for uploading and reading the file).

Step 2: Generating Key

  • Click on the email that’s been created on service accounts.
  • In the Keys tab, Click ‘Add key’. (Create or upload key).
  • Generate and download. (You have an option to download JSON or p12 file).

Note: If your organisation does not allow downloading the JSON key or P12 you will have to change the org policies. To override you should use Tag.

  • Navigate to your Org from ‘Project Picker’.
  • Move to Tag menu.
  • Create a Tag key (eg: EnableServiceKeyCreation). Open the Tag Key that you created now.
  • Create Tag values for the Tag Key. (Eg: true, false).

Attaching the Tag to your Project:

  • Navigate to Manage resources and select your Project.
  • Click on the ‘Tag’ and your Org in ‘Select scope for your tags’.
  • Now in the ‘key 1’ you can see the tag key you created earlier listed. And its corresponding values in the ‘Value1’ drop down.
  • Select whether you want enable/disable the service account creation.

  • Select ‘Organisation Policies’ and select ‘iam.managed.disableServiceAccountCreation’.
  • Click Manage policy.
  • Change to ‘Override Parent Policy’.
  • To override you  need to configure the policy with Rule. One for ‘Enforcement’ and one for ‘Not enforced’
  • Any one rule has to be default.

  • Condition needs to added for any one of the rule. Here in this example, I have added the key and if the tag value  true then the policy will be enforced.
  • Click Save

  • Click ‘Set Policy’. ( you will need Org Policy admin, Org Admin, Org Viewer, cloud asset, Tag Admin roles). Only after this you can download the JSON or P12.

Step 3: Creating Private key alias

P12 file :

  • you can just download the file and a password with be given by the GCS.
  • Upload the file as Key pair in SAP Integration Suite and give a name for the alias and enter the same password that GCS given to you.

JSON File:

  • Extract the private key from the JSON. (Remove the /n and “).
  • Save it as <filename>.pem
  • Create ‘public certificate’
  • Create p12 file
  • Now, upload this p12 file as Key pair in SAP Integration Suite and give the name for alias and enter the same password that you set before.

How to Configure the Adapter:

  • Use the alias we created in the ‘Private Key Alias’ and the service account email that was created in GCS.

How to configure Google Storage Receiver adapter to Upload a file?

  • Operation : Create to upload a file.
  • Directory: Folder path to upload file.
  • Object Name : file name
  • Content type: file content type
  • Existing file handling:
    • override - Creates a new object or replaces the object with new one.
    • Ignore - Skip object creation and no error
    • Fail - throws error

How to Configure the Google Storage Sender to read a file?

  • Directory: Folder to read the file from
  • Object Name: File name to read
  • Post processing: after processing you can Archive or delete or keep the object.

After deploying your Iflow you can see the file is created  / retrieved to/from your specified location in GCS. Explore more on the object where you can do archive or delete the file once it is fetched by the Iflow.

Useful Links: 

SAP Documentation: SAP Google Storage Adapter Guide | SAP Google Storage Adapter Help
